data_models.description: Represents a unique individual interacting with the system, such as a property owner, tenant, or vendor contact. This is distinct from a User, which is an account with login credentials.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the Person.

fields.name: first_name
fields.description: The person's first name.

fields.name: last_name
fields.description: The person's last name.

fields.name: email
fields.description: The person's primary email address. Must be unique.

fields.name: phone_number
fields.description: The person's primary phone number.

relationships.description: A Person can have one User account for system access.
A Person can own or reside in multiple Properties.

data_models.description: Represents a system account with credentials, roles, and permissions, allowing a Person to log in and perform actions.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key, foreign key to Person ID.

fields.name: password_hash
fields.description: Hashed password for authentication.

fields.name: last_login_at
fields.description: Timestamp of the last successful login.

relationships.description: Each User account corresponds to exactly one Person.
A User can have multiple Roles, granting them specific permissions.

invariants: A User must be associated with a Person.

data_models.description: A named set of permissions that can be assigned to Users. Examples: 'Owner', 'Tenant', 'Manager', 'Vendor'.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the Role.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The unique name of the role (e.g., 'Community Manager').

relationships.description: A Role includes a set of specific Permissions.

data_models.description: Defines a specific action that can be performed on a resource (e.g., 'create:work_order', 'view:invoice').

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the Permission.

fields.name: action
fields.description: The action being permitted (e.g., 'create', 'read', 'update', 'delete').

fields.name: resource
fields.description: The system resource this permission applies to (e.g., 'work_order').

data_models.description: Represents a Community Association (HOA), which is a primary tenant in the system. It contains properties, people, and financial accounts.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the Community.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The legal name of the association.

fields.name: address
fields.description: The physical address of the community.

relationships.description: A Community can be part of a larger management Portfolio.
A Community consists of multiple Properties.
Each Community has its own Chart of Accounts.

data_models.description: A physical property or unit within a Community, such as a house or condominium unit.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the Property.

fields.name: address
fields.description: The full address of the property, including unit number.

fields.name: parcel_id
fields.description: Official government parcel identifier.

relationships.description: Each Property belongs to a single Community.
A property can have multiple associated people (owners, tenants).
A property can contain multiple tracked assets.
Service requests are issued for a specific property.

data_models.description: Represents a request for maintenance or repair work on a property, from creation through to completion.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the Work Order.

fields.name: title
fields.description: A brief summary of the issue.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Detailed description of the service requested.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current stage of the work order lifecycle.

fields.name: priority
fields.description: The urgency of the work order, potentially set by AI triage.

relationships.description: The property where service is required.
The person who requested the service.
The service provider assigned to the work.
The invoice generated for the completed work.
Photos or documents related to the work order.
The results of the AI triage process.

invariants: An Invoice can only be created for a Work Order in 'completed' status.

data_models.description: Represents a vendor company that provides services, such as plumbing or electrical work.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the Service Provider.

fields.name: company_name
fields.description: The legal name of the vendor company.

fields.name: contact_email
fields.description: Primary business email.

fields.name: skills
fields.description: A list of skills or service categories (e.g., ['plumbing', 'hvac']).

relationships.description: A service provider employs multiple technicians.
A vendor must provide compliance documents (e.g., insurance).
A vendor can be assigned to many work orders.

data_models.description: Tracks a community rule violation, from initial evidence to resolution and potential fine.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the Violation.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Description of the violation.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current state of the violation.

fields.name: occurred_at
fields.description: When the violation was observed.

relationships.description: The property where the violation occurred.
Evidence for the violation, such as photos.
A fine issued as an invoice for the violation.

invariants: A fine (Invoice) can only be posted after the violation is in an appropriate state (e.g., 'resolved' with fine).

data_models.description: Manages the submission, review, and approval process for architectural change requests (ARC) within a community.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the ARC request.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the proposed change.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Detailed description of the project.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current status of the request.

relationships.description: The property for which the change is requested.
The homeowner submitting the request.
Supporting documents, such as plans or surveys.

data_models.description: An account in the General Ledger (Chart of Accounts), such as 'Cash', 'Accounts Receivable', or 'Repair Expense'.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the GL Account.

fields.name: account_number
fields.description: The unique number for the account.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the account.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of account, determines its behavior in financial statements.

relationships.description: Each GL Account belongs to a specific Community's Chart of Accounts.

data_models.description: An auditable, immutable financial transaction in the General Ledger, consisting of balanced debit and credit entries.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the transaction.

fields.name: transaction_date
fields.description: The date the transaction occurred.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A description of the financial event.

fields.name: entries
fields.description: An array of debit and credit entries, where {account_id, amount, type: 'debit'|'credit'}.

invariants: The sum of all debit entries must equal the sum of all credit entries for the transaction to be valid.

data_models.description: A bill for services rendered or a fine. It tracks amount due, payment status, and links to the GL.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the Invoice.

fields.name: amount
fields.description: The total amount due, in cents.

fields.name: due_date
fields.description: The date by which the invoice must be paid.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current status of the invoice.

relationships.description: Invoice for a completed Work Order.
Invoice for a violation fine.
An invoice can be paid via one or more payments.

invariants: Invoice status must be 'open' to accept payments.

data_models.description: Represents any uploaded file, such as a photo, PDF, or document, with metadata and storage information.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the Digital Asset.

fields.name: file_name
fields.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fields.name: mime_type
fields.description: The MIME type of the file.

fields.name: storage_path
fields.description: The path or key to the file in object storage.

data_models.description: Stores the output from the AI triage process for a given Work Order, including classification and summarization.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the triage result.

fields.name: work_order_id
fields.description: Foreign key to the Work Order being triaged.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: AI-generated summary of the issue.

fields.name: classification
fields.description: AI-determined classification (e.g., category, priority).

relationships.description: Each triage result corresponds to one Work Order.

data_models.description: A record of a single payment transaction made towards an invoice.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the payment record.

fields.name: amount
fields.description: The amount paid, in cents.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The status of the payment transaction.

fields.name: processed_at
fields.description: When the payment was processed.

relationships.description: The invoice this payment is for.
The payment method used for this transaction.

data_models.description: An individual service technician who is an employee of a Service Provider.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the Technician.

fields.name: person_id
fields.description: Link to the Person record for this technician.

relationships.description: The company that employs the technician.
The technician's work schedule and availability.

invariants: A technician must be associated with a Service Provider.

data_models.description: Represents a targeted message sent to a specific user or group of users about a system event.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the notification.

fields.name: recipient_id
fields.description: The ID of the User receiving the notification.

fields.name: content
fields.description: The message content of the notification.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The delivery status of the notification.

fields.name: sent_at
fields.description: When the notification was sent.

data_models.description: An immutable log of significant events and changes made within the system, for security and compliance.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key for the audit log entry.

fields.name: user_id
fields.description: The user who performed the action. Null if system action.

fields.name: action
fields.description: A description of the action performed (e.g., 'user.login.success', 'work_order.update.status').

fields.name: details
fields.description: Additional details about the event, such as the data that was changed.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Timestamp of when the event occurred.
